Works together to garner community engagement and input towards reducing infant and maternal mortality and morbidity rates for Black/African American Families.
Share:
Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health
Black Infant Health Community Advisory Board
The mission of the Black Infant Health Community Advisory Board is to garner community engagement and input in the effort to reduce infant and maternal mortality and morbidity rates in Black/African American families by providing collective strategies that reduce barriers to whole person and perinatal care, as well as advocate around health issues relating to the Black/African American community.
4-image collage of diverse family units
How to Enroll:
To join the Advisory Board, please complete the
Member Application Form
To be a participant of the Black Infant Health Program, call
(707) 553-5055
Visit the
Baby First Solano
website
Meeting Schedule:
We meet four (4) times per year, from 1:00-3:00pm, during the 3rd week of the following months:
February
May
August
November
How to be Engaged:
Solano County Black Infant Health Facebook Page:
https://www.facebook.com/SolanoCountyBlackInfantHealthProgram

Together, we can make a difference to help ensure that all babies are born healthy!
